title: Retire GitHub Actions workflows (benchmark-ci.yml, e2e.yml) - Argo CI covers both
description:
Policy: GitHub Actions are disabled across all repos; all CI runs on Argo Workflows (iad-ci). This repo still carries two workflow files under .github/workflows/ that contradict that:

1. benchmark-ci.yml - triggers on push/pull_request to main and runs the fusion-loop timing benchmark. This gate ALREADY runs in the spaxel-build Argo WorkflowTemplate (go test -bench=BenchmarkFusionLoop step), so the GH workflow is redundant dead config that suggests re-enabling Actions.
2. e2e.yml - workflow_dispatch/repository_dispatch trigger meant to be fired by Argo; superseded by the spaxel-e2e Argo WorkflowTemplate which runs the acceptance/e2e suites directly in-cluster.

Steps:
- Delete both files under .github/workflows/ (or the whole .github/workflows dir if nothing else remains).
- Update docs/ci-benchmark-integration.md: remove the 'GitHub Actions Integration' section (it recommends 'gh workflow run benchmark-ci.yml') and keep it Argo-only; note the benchmark step already exists in the spaxel-build template.
- Grep the repo for other references to these workflows (README-level docs, PROGRESS.md) and clean them up.

Acceptance criteria:
- No *.yml remains under .github/workflows/ that has push/pull_request triggers
- docs/ci-benchmark-integration.md no longer references GitHub Actions as a runnable path
- No functional CI change needed in declarative-config (the Argo templates already cover both jobs)

title: Add root README.md (repo standard, GitHub mirror landing page)
description:
The repo has NO README.md at the root (only dashboard/README.md exists). The workspace repo standard requires a root README.md explaining the repo's purpose and contents, and the GitHub mirror (github.com/jedarden/spaxel) renders an empty landing page without it.

Write a root README.md covering:
- What Spaxel is (WiFi CSI-based indoor positioning for self-hosted homes; single Docker 'mothership' container + ESP32-S3 node fleet) — source the overview from docs/plan/plan.md System Overview and 'What Spaxel Can Realistically Achieve' sections
- Repo layout: mothership/ (Go backend, go.work module), cmd/sim (CSI simulator CLI module), firmware/ (ESP-IDF C project), dashboard/ (vanilla JS + Three.js UI), test/acceptance (acceptance test module), docs/ (plan, notes, research)
- Quickstart: docker-compose.yml usage, key env vars (SPAXEL_BIND_ADDR, SPAXEL_DATA_DIR, SPAXEL_MQTT_BROKER) per plan Deployment section
- Pointers to docs/plan/plan.md, docs/notes/, docs/research/, dashboard/README.md
- Realistic-capability and privacy framing (CSI-only, no camera/audio, self-hosted, no cloud)

Acceptance criteria:
- README.md exists at repo root, accurate to the CURRENT tree (go.work multi-module layout, image ronaldraygun/spaxel), no secrets, no absolute filesystem paths
- Under ~150 lines; links resolve to files that actually exist

title: Add missing fuzz/property tests: phase sanitize, replay seek, auth VerifyToken
description:
docs/plan/plan.md 'Testing Strategy > Property-Based / Fuzz Tests' lists five high-value targets. Only the two ingestion ones exist today (files frame_fuzz_test.go and json_fuzz_test.go, in the ingestion package under mothership/internal/). Missing three:

1. Pipeline phase sanitization - plan wants a table-driven property test over edge I/Q pairs (all-zero, max int8, alternating sign); property: output is always finite (no NaN/Inf). Code lives in the signal package under mothership/internal/ (phase.go) - confirm exact package and exported function first.
2. Replay seek-to-timestamp - fuzz with arbitrary int64 timestamps before/after file bounds and at wrap points; property: no panic, returned frame (if any) has recv_time_ms >= target. Code: replay package under mothership/internal/.
3. Auth token verification - tokens with wrong length, invalid hex, correct length but wrong bytes; property: never panics. Code: auth package under mothership/internal/.

Conventions (match existing ingestion fuzz tests):
- Fuzz targets in files named *_fuzz_test.go in the owning package
- Seed corpus entries so plain 'go test ./...' exercises seeds quickly; long fuzzing only via 'go test -fuzz' (plan: excluded from regular runs to avoid indefinite CI loops)
- The phase-sanitization one may be a deterministic table-driven property test rather than a native fuzz target, per the plan table

Acceptance criteria:
- Three new test files as above; 'go test ./...' in mothership/ stays green and fast (no fuzzing loops in default mode)
- Each fuzz target runs at least 10s locally under 'go test -fuzz' without findings, or found bugs are fixed in the same change

title: Wire dashboard jest unit test suite into CI (only a11y runs today)
description:
The dashboard has a substantial jest unit test suite (10+ *.test.js files under dashboard/js/, e.g. ambient.test.js, replay.test.js, fresnel.test.js, command palette/fleet-page/explainability tests; jest config at dashboard/jest.config.js; script 'test': 'jest --verbose' in dashboard/package.json). But the CI pipeline (spaxel-build Argo WorkflowTemplate in the jedarden/declarative-config repo, path k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/, spaxel-build template) only runs 'npm run test:a11y' (playwright axe-core gate). The jest suite is never executed in CI, so dashboard JS regressions ship silently.

Steps:
1. Run the jest suite locally from dashboard/ ('npm ci && npm test') and fix any currently-failing tests first (the suite may have bit-rotted precisely because it is unwired).
2. Add a 'npm test' (jest) invocation to the a11y/dashboard test step of the spaxel-build WorkflowTemplate in the declarative-config repo (same container/step that already does npm ci + playwright), ordered before the playwright a11y run so cheap unit failures fail fast.
3. Commit the declarative-config change per that repo's conventions (ArgoCD app argo-workflows-ns-iad-ci syncs on push).

Acceptance criteria:
- 'npm test' passes locally in dashboard/
- spaxel-build template runs jest and fails the workflow on jest failure
- a11y gate behavior unchanged
Note: work spans two repos (this repo for any test fixes, declarative-config for the CI wiring). GitHub Actions must NOT be used - Argo only.

title: Populate link count in calibration_complete WebSocket event
description: mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go line 1438 sends links: 0 hardcoded in the calibration_complete WebSocket message. Should pass the actual count of active links from the ingestion server (ingestion.Server.ActiveLinkCount or similar). Affects the guided troubleshooting dashboard display after calibration.

title: Update docs/plan/plan.md to reflect implemented reality (stale sections)
description:
docs/plan/plan.md header says 'Last updated: 2026-05-24, Status: COMPLETE' but several sections no longer match the tree (repo is now at VERSION 0.1.357 with ongoing fixes). Single consolidated plan-refresh task — do NOT split further.

Stale sections to fix in docs/plan/plan.md:
1. 'Go Module Layout' section: plan shows a single root module (cmd/mothership, internal/, test/integration/). Reality: go.work at root with three modules — mothership/ (contains cmd/mothership, internal/), cmd/sim, test/acceptance. There is no test/integration/ directory; simulator-based tests live in test/acceptance/ and mothership/test/acceptance/.
2. 'Deployment > Dockerfile' section: plan shows FROM golang:1.23 building ./cmd/mothership and image ghcr.io/spaxel/spaxel. Reality (see Dockerfile at repo root): espressif/idf:v5.2 firmware build stage + golang:1.25-bookworm, GOOS/GOARCH pinned to linux/amd64 (single-arch, deliberate), image published as ronaldraygun/spaxel via the spaxel-build Argo WorkflowTemplate.
3. 'Quality Gates / Definition of Done' item 4 requires linux/amd64+linux/arm64 multi-arch buildx. Actual CI builds amd64 only. Update the gate to document the amd64-only decision (deployment target is amd64 k8s), or mark arm64 as future work — do not silently leave the contradiction.
4. 'Testing Strategy > Integration Tests' says tests are 'Located in test/integration/' — correct to actual locations (test/acceptance/, mothership/test/acceptance/, tests/e2e/run.sh).
5. 'Open Questions' has a duplicated 'Multi-installation coordination' bullet (appears twice back-to-back at the end) — deduplicate.
6. Bump the 'Last updated' date and adjust the Status line (e.g. 'COMPLETE — maintenance mode') to reflect that beads referenced ('83 beads closed') were archived.

Acceptance criteria:
- All six items above corrected in docs/plan/plan.md; no other plan content rewritten
- Every path named in the updated sections exists in the repo
- Do not touch PROGRESS.md (separate bead bf-833 covers it)

title: Fix hardcoded version string in api/status.go
description: mothership/internal/api/status.go line 98 returns hardcoded version '1.0.0' instead of the actual build version. Wire the VERSION file or build-time ldflags version into the StatusHandler so GET /api/status returns the real deployed version (currently 0.1.357+).

title: Update PROGRESS.md: mark Phase 2 pending items as complete
description: Phase 2 table shows three items as Pending but all are done. Presence indicator is in live.html and app.js. CSI recording buffer is in mothership/internal/recording/buffer.go. Adaptive sensing rate is in mothership/internal/ingestion/ratecontrol.go. Mark all three Done in PROGRESS.md.

title: Add firmware host-based unit tests (nvs migration, CSI frame serialization, provisioning parser)
description:
docs/plan/plan.md 'Testing Strategy > Firmware Tests (host-based unit tests)' mandates host tests for three firmware modules, but firmware/ contains NO test files at all.

Required per plan:
- nvs — NVS schema migration: simulate schema_ver=0 to 1 upgrade (source: firmware/main/nvs_migration.c/.h)
- csi — binary frame serialization: verify frame header fields and little-endian encoding (source: firmware/main/csi.c/.h; the 24-byte binary frame format is specified in docs/plan/plan.md Component 1/2 and mirrored by the Go parser in mothership/internal/ingestion/)
- serial_prov — provisioning JSON parser: valid JSON parsed correctly; invalid JSON returns {"ok":false} (source: firmware/main/provision.c/.h)

Implementation guidance:
- Preferred: ESP-IDF host-based testing (idf.py --target linux / Unity test framework) as the plan specifies. If the ESP-IDF linux target proves impractical for these files (heavy esp_* dependencies), an acceptable alternative is extracting the pure-logic parts (frame packing, JSON parse, migration decision table) into functions testable from a plain C host harness compiled with gcc, kept under firmware/test/.
- Keep tests deterministic; no hardware or network required.
- Document how to run them in a short section of the test file header or firmware/test/README-less comment block (do not create new top-level docs).

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests exist under firmware/ (e.g. firmware/test/) covering all three modules above with at least: one schema_ver 0-to-1 migration case, one little-endian frame round-trip case incl. header fields, one valid + one invalid provisioning JSON case
- A single documented command runs them on a Linux host and exits non-zero on failure
- Existing firmware build (firmware/CMakeLists.txt, Dockerfile firmware stage) still builds unchanged

title: Repo hygiene: remove stray tracked artifacts and reconcile duplicate test/sim trees
description:
Several stray artifacts are tracked in git, and the test/simulator trees are duplicated in confusing ways. Careful: CI templates in the jedarden/declarative-config repo (spaxel-build and spaxel-e2e WorkflowTemplates under k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/) reference specific paths - map their references FIRST and only remove what nothing uses.

Known strays (tracked in git):
- fix_ble_handlers.py at repo root - one-off fixup script, not part of the product; delete it
- dashboard/test-results/.last-run.json - playwright run artifact; delete and add dashboard/test-results/ to .gitignore
- mothership/cmd/_parse_check.go - stray scratch file next to real cmds; verify unused and delete

Duplicated trees to reconcile (do NOT delete blindly - check CI references first):
- cmd/sim (own go.mod, listed in go.work) vs mothership/cmd/sim - two simulator sources. spaxel-build builds ./cmd/sim from the mothership module dir; determine which is canonical, delete or clearly de-duplicate the other, update go.work if the root module goes away
- test/acceptance (own go.mod, in go.work; files as1_setup_test.go etc.) vs mothership/test/acceptance (files as1_first_time_setup_test.go etc.) - both are exercised by spaxel-e2e template at different steps; if both are intentionally kept (module-external vs in-module suites), add a short comment/doc line in each explaining the split instead of deleting
- tests/e2e/run.sh vs mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go - same: identify which CI path uses each, document or consolidate

Acceptance criteria:
- The three stray files above are gone from git tracking; .gitignore covers playwright artifacts and local binaries (mothership/mothership, mothership/sim if not already ignored)
- Every remaining test/sim tree is either referenced by CI or by go.work, and each carries a one-line purpose note (file comment) so the duplication is explainable
- go build ./... and go test ./... still succeed in the mothership module; go.work remains consistent
- No CI template edits required, OR if a path must change, the corresponding declarative-config template is updated in the same effort
